nodes.title: Safety Management Requirements for Defence Systems

Requirements for contractor safety management system
Safety Requirements, Hazard and Risk Analysis
Monitoring, Reporting and In-service Data Analysis
This standard specifies the requirements for achieving, assuring and managing the safety of PSS defined by the scope of contract.
The contractor, together with the UK MOD, has responsibility for safety of all deliverable PSS.
The responsibility of the contractor also varies with the scope of analysis.
Whilst contract life might be limited, this standard considers the whole life cycle of the PSS, including disposal.
ASEMS DE&S Acquisition Safety and Environmental Management System

An Introduction to Safety Management in the UK MOD (White Book)
The contractor shall define an SMP as part of the tendering process
The contractor shall agree their SMP with the UK MOD
The contractor shall review and update the SMP
The contractor shall review the SMP on a regular basis
Changes required to the SMP shall be agreed with the UK MOD
Ensure all changes in design are managed in accordance with the SMP
The contractor shall report progress against the SMP
Ensure progress reports highlight all safety issues
Legislation, Regulations, Standards, Policy and Approved Codes of Practice
UK MOD POSMS Project Safety Initiation procedures
The contractor shall preserve the information set for the period or periods specified in the contract and as required by law.
The contractor shall ensure that the information set remains accessible as techniques, methodologies and tools change, through the life of the contract.
The contractor shall pass information in an easily accessible form to the UK MOD, regulators and any other organisations identified in the contract.
The contractor shall define the information set such that all information that might have a safety role are included.
The contractor shall define, and agree with the UK MOD, processes for information management.
Where the contractor anticipates difficulties, or very high costs, in preserving access to parts of the information set they shall discuss approaches and options with the UK MOD.
The contractor shall consider obsolescence of the technologies used for preserving the information set.
The contractor shall consider security risks to the information set to ensure its confidentiality, integrity and availability can be maintained.
The contractor shall produce documentary deliverables relevant to safety, including interim versions, as contracted.
The contractor shall agree with the UK MOD the format and content for all contracted safety-related deliverables in the scope of supply.
In defining deliverable formatting and content, the contractor shall take into account the DIDs and the requirements of any civil, open or other standards being used.
The contractor shall develop and update the deliverable plans, reports and summaries
The contractor shall agree with the UK MOD, the PSS and safety-related documentation
The contractor shall define the PSS and its given application
The contractor shall define the PSS to include all relevant elements across the DLOD
The contractor shall record the definition of the PSS
The contractor shall define the roles and responsibilities of those individuals responsible for safety
The contractor shall identify the normal point of contact for safety matters
The contractor shall demonstrate how responsibility is delegated
The contractor shall keep the definition of roles and responsibilities up to date
The contractor shall identify and record competency requirements for each role
The contractor shall contribute to safety committees and other liaison activities
The contractor shall provide visibility of the information set to the safety committee
The contractor shall support the safety committee in recommending, endorsing or providing guidance
The contractor shall support the safety committee in any additional roles/tasks
Where there is an appropriate UK MOD safety committee, the contractor shall participate
Where there is no appropriate safety committee, the contractor shall work with the UK MOD
Where appropriate the safety committee may delegate work to subsidiary committees
Where the contract involves support, the contractor shall ensure the safety committee approves changes
The contractor shall record all their support given to the safety committee
The contractor shall ensure that all safety-relevant tasks are carried out by competent individuals
The contractor shall record the evidence of competence
The contractor shall undertake competence management
Contractors shall ensure that competent personnel are appointed
The contractor shall ensure competence evidence is updated
The contractor shall cooperate, and coordinate safety activities
The contractor shall identify all stakeholders relevant to safety
The contractor shall ensure timely and accurate communication
The contractor shall record all assumptions and information
The contractor shall identify and record assumptions about PSS
The contractor shall record assumptions for other organisations
The contractor shall define and manage the technical interfaces
IET Code of Practice for Competence for Safety-Related Systems Practitioners (COPCSRSP)
UK MOD safety-related competence through the KID
The contractor shall ensure the CSA is independent
IET Code of Practice for Independent Safety Assessors (COPISA)
The contractor shall allow an ISA reasonable access
Restrictions on access to information set
Identify and implement timely remedial actions
Agree remedial actions with the UK MOD
Update the SMP to reflect agreed remedial actions
Identify all hazards and potential accidents
Consider human factors in hazard analysis
Identification and Tracking of Safety Requirements
The contractor shall ensure that the safety analysis results remain consistent with the design.
The contractor shall identify all potential failure modes that might contribute to a hazard in the PSS, or in any known interfacing or interacting PSS, whether extant or planned.
The contractor shall ensure that the status of control of all identified failure modes that contribute to a hazard is visible throughout the contract.
The contractor shall include, in the information set, information about all identified failure modes.
The contractor shall include in the ISSS, information on the status of all identified failure modes.
The contractor shall estimate the likelihood of occurrence and opportunities for mitigation for all identified failure modes that contribute to a hazard and record the results in the information set.
The contractor shall identify and justify in the information set the units used for likelihood of occurrence estimates.
The contractor shall employ systematic safety analysis processes for identification of failure modes which might contribute to a hazard in the PSS as defined in the SMP.
The contractor shall ensure that human factors are considered where they might be a contributory cause of a failure mode.
The contractor shall ensure that cyber security is considered where intentional or unintentional unauthorised electronic interactions might be a contributory cause of a failure mode.
The contractor shall ensure that systematic failures are considered where they might be a contributory cause of a failure mode.
The contractor shall ensure that the undesired impacts of normal functions are considered; this is especially important for contractors carrying out systems integration.

The contractor shall update information on identified failure modes throughout the contract to ensure that it accurately reflects the status of the design, safety analysis and safety engineering activities.
The contractor shall use qualitative estimates where it is not practicable to quantify likelihood.

The contractor shall identify potential mitigations for the failure modes that contribute to a hazard where this is practicable; in particular they shall identify any observable attributes of the PSS which could be used as triggers for mitigations.
The contractor shall consider human factors where they might provide risk mitigation.
The contractor shall consider cyber security controls where they might provide risk mitigation.
The contractor shall record the results of the failure mode assessment in the information set.
The contractor shall record all assumptions, data, judgements and calculations underpinning the failure mode assessment in the information set.
The contractor shall carry out safety and systems engineering activities to provide evidence that all safety requirements, including derived safety requirements, have been met.
The contractor shall undertake systems engineering activities which are capable of detecting counter-evidence.
The contractor shall identify the most effective method, or methods, for showing satisfaction of requirements, or providing counter-evidence, and include this information in the SMP.

Whilst there might be general identification of techniques early in the process, the contractor shall ensure that the methods chosen are appropriate to the specific derived safety requirements identified.
The contractor shall produce an ISSS as defined in the SMP.
The contractor shall ensure that the ISSS contains sufficient information from the information set to enable a system integrator and a system operator to discharge their safety responsibilities.
The contractor shall ensure that the ISSS contains information on assumptions and limitations regarding the safe use of the PSS.
The contractor shall ensure that the ISSS includes a justification of the scope of the information provided.
The contractor shall ensure that the ISSS is delivered incrementally, as contracted and as defined in the SMP, to give the UK MOD visibility of progress in safety engineering and safety analysis.
The contractor shall produce a safety case or safety cases for a PSS as defined in the SMP.

The contractor shall ensure that the safety case consists of a structured argument, supported by a body of evidence that provides a compelling, comprehensible and valid case that a system is safe for a given application in a given environment.
The contractor shall ensure that the evidence for the safety case is drawn from the information set.
The contractor shall address safety throughout the life of the PSS within the safety case, to the extent required by the scope of contract.
The contractor shall provide evidence that supports claims for the verification and validation of all safety requirements, including evidence from sub-contractors.
The contractor shall provide evidence of compliance with relevant legislation, regulations, standards, policy and approved codes of practice.
The contractor shall ensure that the safety case identifies how to address any residual shortfalls in meeting safety requirements.
The contractor shall develop, maintain and refine the safety case as defined in the SMP.
The contractor shall provide diverse evidence, to ensure that that the overall safety argument is not compromised by errors or uncertainties in individual pieces of evidence.
The contractor shall justify the adequacy and suitability of the methods and techniques used for hazard analysis, design for safety, safety analysis and other relevant safety engineering activities.
The contractor shall produce a safety case report or reports as defined in the SMP.
The contractor shall produce safety case reports that incorporate the key elements of the safety argument and provision of evidence.
Where there are shortfalls in the evidence the contractor shall ensure safety case reports provide the rationale for operating the PSS, and the ways of mitigating the residual risk.
The contractor shall ensure that safety case reports contain information on assumptions and limitations regarding the safe use of the PSS.
The contractor shall manage all changes under their control...
The contractor shall review and update the information set...
The contractor shall review and update the safety case...
The contractor shall update the design where there are agreed changes...
The contractor shall update the hazard analysis and safety analysis...
Following any change which has an adverse effect on safety...
The contractor shall supply updated PSS and associated information...
The contractor shall supply appropriate installation instructions...
The contractor shall update the build state definition...
The contractor shall monitor changes to in-service PSS...
Where undesired safety impacts are identified...
The contractor shall incorporate any new or modified PSS...
The contractor shall provide information to other relevant stakeholders...
The contractor shall assess supplied PSS...
Monitoring, Reporting and In-service Data Analysis
The contractor shall define and operate a process for collecting, analysing and documenting safety-relevant in-service data
The contractor shall review the safety case in light of the recorded data
The contractor shall sentence the results of analysis of the data
The contractor shall inform all relevant stakeholders where they have identified the need for remedial action
The contractor shall define and operate a process for collecting and analysing incident, accident and near miss reports
The contractor shall analyse all collected data to identify root causes which require action
The contractor shall monitor and report changes to relevant safety legislation
The contractor shall liaise with the UK MOD to establish a Failure Reporting Analysis and Corrective Action Systems (FRACAS)
Where the contractor supports in-service PSS which are in use by multiple stakeholders
The contractor shall implement remedial actions to preserve or improve safety
The contractor shall plan remedial actions taking into account the need for efficient change management
The contractor shall plan remedial actions taking into account the need to deal with foreseeable changes
The contractor shall produce a safety case report and command summary and deliver them to the UK MOD for approval before commencement of services.
The contractor shall maintain the safety case, safety case report and command summary so they are accurate representations of the service.
The contractor shall produce command summaries so that each provision of the service can be properly assessed and controlled in terms of risk.
The contractor shall provide information to support domain specific processes providing essential information for the accountable person responsible for the service to manage risk to life.
The contractor shall produce plans for management of service operations, covering all reasonably foreseeable situations including abnormal and emergency situations.

The contractor shall ensure that the plans cover the safety of the full range of normal services and operations, including but not limited to defining standard operating procedures, resourcing, training, and oversight arrangements.

The contractor shall ensure that the plans cover the safety of emergency situations, including but not limited to defining emergency response, coordination and decision making, including liaison with the service accountable person and relevant stakeholders.

The contractor shall ensure that these plans cover safe update, including ways of making changes on continuously running systems, if necessary, building on installation instructions supplied from support, as appropriate.
The contractor shall ensure that the communications plan, detailed in the SMP, includes processes for delivery of in-service data and build state definition.

The contractor shall provide information to those not employed as part of the provision of the service about relevant undertakings, where this might affect their health and safety. How this will be achieved shall be included in the relevant plans.
The contractor shall provide access to health and safety advice relating to the service, and consultation on the risks to employees, contractors and any other people who could be affected by them.
The contractor shall support the UK MOD in managing predicted or emergent risk to life
The contractor shall cooperate with the accountable persons for interfacing or interacting services
Where necessary and with the accountable person’s agreement, the contractor shall implement immediate action
